Topic 4: Iran update part 1 (breaking the stalemate & opening the Strait)

[189:50]And the big story tonight is how the war is now escalating.

[189:54]And the situation is this,

[189:57]as we have talked about,

[189:58]we are halfway through the third week in the war.

[190:01]We expected, well, I don't even know what we expected.

[190:06]You can't even really use that word.

[190:08]Perhaps the president alone expected

[190:11]that this would be a quick and easy war.

[190:14]Pound Iran very hard for a few days to capitate the leadership and a crippled and wounded

[190:21]Iranian regime will very quickly wave the white flag and sue for peace, whereupon the

[190:29]Trump administration can impose conditions on Iran's nuclear program.

[190:33]That was the idea.

[190:35]As you know, things did not go according to Trump's plan.

[190:39]The Iranian regime survived.

[190:42]The Iranian regime maintains its capability

[190:44]to launch drones and ballistic missiles.

[190:47]And they have used that capability to attack

[190:50]the Persian Gulf nations of the Gulf Cooperation Council

[190:54]to close the Strait of Hormuz, a vital choke point,

[190:58]for oil, natural gas, and fertilizer,

[191:03]and to attack and degrade US radar early warning systems and to attack the state of Israel.

[191:11]And so the war has settled into a second phase, you can say, if the first phase was the US

[191:21]Bombing runs thousands of times in the first 12, 24 hours.

[191:26]Bombing regime targets, decapitation strikes,

[191:30]bombing their Navy, their military hardware

[191:33]and infrastructure, logistics, supply chains,

[191:37]things like that.

[191:38]Now the war is settled into a second phase

[191:40]where it has become an attritional war.

[191:42]And it has become a war of economics.

[191:45]The United States and Israel are using their air and sea power to bomb Iran all the time

[191:50]and continues to decimate its leadership, continues to find and destroy its missile production

[191:56]and missile launch capabilities.

[191:59]But, as long as Iran remains intact and as long as they're able to launch drones and

[192:04]missiles, then they are able to shut down shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, which chokes

[192:08]the global energy supply, hurting the global economy, and they're able to

[192:14]increase the pressure on the Gulf nations

[192:16]by hurting their real estate, tourism,

[192:19]and other industries which they're trying to pivot towards.

[192:22]So this is the second phase.

[192:24]This is the grinding stalemate.

[192:26]This is the status quo, attritional war

[192:29]that the conflict has settled into as we've talked about.

[192:33]And so the question facing the Trump administration is how to break the stalemate.

[192:39]It can't go on forever.

[192:41]The longer that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, there is going to be a lot of damage to the

[192:46]global economy.

[192:47]It might even trigger a global recession.

[192:50]Energy becomes scarce, oil prices increase, and that increases the price of everything.

[192:56]Fertilizer supply is constrained, that makes costs for farmers go up, that makes food more expensive.

[193:03]So we are facing higher inflation.

[193:07]There was an expectation the Federal Reserve, for example, would cut interest rates today.

[193:11]They're not able to do that because actually mortgages are going up.

[193:17]Prices are going up.

[193:18]Inflation appears to be ticking higher in anticipation.

[193:21]The economy is pricing in the long term risks to the oil supply because of the conflict.

[193:28]So Trump is looking for a way out here.

[193:30]He's looking to break the stalemate, and there's, as we talked about, a couple of ways to do

[193:35]this.

[193:36]Either we have a truce and a ceasefire.

[193:39]And that allows the Strait of Hormuz to open up

[193:42]and it relieves the economic pressure

[193:44]on the United States and the world.

[193:46]So that's one way to break the stalemate

[193:47]is effectively a strategic retreat and withdrawal.

[193:52]The other way is we have to actually win.

[193:57]but this is a very complicated endeavor

Topic 5: Iran update part 2 (escalation, Europe's turndown & possible energy crisis)

[201:59]an island in the Persian Gulf off the coast of Iran. That is where 90% of Iran's oil exports move

[202:07]Iran gets the oil on the coastline and in the Persian Gulf,

[202:10]refines it on the Iranian mainland,

[202:14]and then ships it out to the island via pipeline

[202:17]where it gets picked up by tankers

[202:19]and then taken out of the Persian Gulf

[202:22]and through the Strait of Hormuz.

[202:24]So this is the hub for virtually all of Iran's oil.

[202:29]This is the critical center of Iran's energy sector.

[202:34]And Trump bombed it on Friday, but only the military targets.

[202:38]Now on Friday, I speculated that maybe that was in preparation for a invasion, for a ground

[202:56]And so you can deprive them of the revenue

[202:59]without actually setting back in a permanent way

[203:06]which would take years to rebuild.
